questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using semantic HTML elements instead of generic elements like div or span?
options: Improves accessibility, SEO, and code maintainability
Reduces file size significantly
Automatically applies better default styles
Improves JavaScript execution speed
questions.correctAnswer: Improves accessibility, SEO, and code maintainability

questions.question: Which description best explains the difference between inline, block, and inline-block elements?
options: Inline elements can have width and height, while block elements cannot
Block elements start on a new line, inline elements flow with text, and inline-block allows sizing while staying inline
Inline-block elements are deprecated and should not be used
There is no meaningful difference between them in modern CSS
questions.correctAnswer: Block elements start on a new line, inline elements flow with text, and inline-block allows sizing while staying inline

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes how CSS positioning works?
options: Relative positioning moves an element relative to its parent, while absolute is relative to the viewport
Relative keeps normal flow, absolute positions relative to the nearest positioned ancestor, fixed stays relative to viewport, and sticky switches between relative and fixed
Absolute positioning always places elements on top of others and fixed elements scroll normally
Sticky positioning is no longer supported in modern browsers
questions.correctAnswer: Relative keeps normal flow, absolute positions relative to the nearest positioned ancestor, fixed stays relative to viewport, and sticky switches between relative and fixed

questions.question: When should ARIA attributes be used in a web application?
options: As a replacement for all semantic HTML elements
Only when native HTML elements do not provide sufficient accessibility
To improve CSS styling capabilities
To define responsive breakpoints in HTML
questions.correctAnswer: Only when native HTML elements do not provide sufficient accessibility

questions.question: Why are relative units like rem, em, and percentages often preferred over pixels in CSS?
options: They scale based on context, improving responsiveness and accessibility
They are processed faster by browsers
They are required for flexbox layouts
They prevent layout shifts entirely
questions.correctAnswer: They scale based on context, improving responsiveness and accessibility

questions.question: Which approach to applying CSS is generally preferred in production environments?
options: Inline styles for faster rendering
Internal stylesheets for better organization
External stylesheets for maintainability and caching
Using only !important rules to enforce consistency
questions.correctAnswer: External stylesheets for maintainability and caching

questions.question: Why is overusing !important considered a bad practice in CSS?
options: It prevents CSS from loading properly
It breaks the cascade and makes styles difficult to maintain and debug
It reduces browser compatibility
It disables media queries
questions.correctAnswer: It breaks the cascade and makes styles difficult to maintain and debug

questions.question: How do viewport units like vh and vw differ from percentages in CSS?
options: Viewport units are based on the browser window size, while percentages depend on the parent element
Percentages are based on the viewport, while vh and vw are fixed values
They behave identically in all layout contexts
Viewport units only apply to font sizes
questions.correctAnswer: Viewport units are based on the browser window size, while percentages depend on the parent element

questions.question: What is the key difference between content-box and border-box in the CSS box model?
options: Content-box includes padding and border in the width, while border-box does not
Border-box includes content, padding, and border within the declared width, while content-box does not
Content-box is only used in flexbox layouts
Border-box is deprecated in modern CSS
questions.correctAnswer: Border-box includes content, padding, and border within the declared width, while content-box does not

questions.question: Why is maintaining a proper heading hierarchy (h1 through h6) important in HTML?
options: It only affects visual styling of text
It improves accessibility, SEO, and provides meaningful document structure
It is required for CSS grid layouts to function
It ensures JavaScript event listeners work correctly
questions.correctAnswer: It improves accessibility, SEO, and provides meaningful document structure

questions.question: What is the difference between var, let, and const in JavaScript?
options: var is function-scoped while let and const are block-scoped, and const cannot be reassigned
All three are block-scoped but const is immutable
let is global while var and const are local
const can be reassigned but let cannot
questions.correctAnswer: var is function-scoped while let and const are block-scoped, and const cannot be reassigned

questions.question: What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function that has access to its own scope, the outer function’s scope, and the global scope
A function that only has access to global variables
A way to stop a function from executing
A method used to close event listeners
questions.correctAnswer: A function that has access to its own scope, the outer function’s scope, and the global scope

questions.question: What will happen if you try to access a variable before it is declared with let or const?
options: It will return undefined
It will throw a ReferenceError due to the temporal dead zone
It will automatically initialize to null
It will behave the same as var
questions.correctAnswer: It will throw a ReferenceError due to the temporal dead zone

questions.question: What is the difference between == and === in JavaScript?
options: == compares both value and type, while === compares only value
=== compares both value and type, while == performs type coercion before comparison
There is no difference between them
== is deprecated in modern JavaScript
questions.correctAnswer: === compares both value and type, while == performs type coercion before comparison

questions.question: How does the JavaScript event loop handle asynchronous operations?
options: It executes async code immediately before synchronous code
It pushes async callbacks to the call stack directly
It moves completed async tasks from the callback queue to the call stack when it is empty
It blocks execution until async tasks are finished
questions.correctAnswer: It moves completed async tasks from the callback queue to the call stack when it is empty

questions.question: What is the purpose of the this keyword in JavaScript?
options: It always refers to the global object
It refers to the object that is currently executing the function
It refers to the parent function scope
It always refers to the DOM element
questions.correctAnswer: It refers to the object that is currently executing the function

questions.question: What is the difference between null and undefined in JavaScript?
options: null is an assigned value representing no value, while undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned
undefined is an assigned value, while null means not declared
They are completely interchangeable
null is automatically assigned by JavaScript in all cases
questions.correctAnswer: null is an assigned value representing no value, while undefined means a variable has been declared but not assigned

questions.question: What does Array.prototype.map do?
options: It modifies the original array directly
It creates a new array by applying a function to each element
It filters elements out of an array
It sorts the array in place
questions.correctAnswer: It creates a new array by applying a function to each element

questions.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous code execution?
options: Synchronous code runs in parallel while asynchronous code runs sequentially
Synchronous code blocks execution until complete, while asynchronous code allows other operations to run before completion
Asynchronous code always runs faster than synchronous code
There is no difference in JavaScript
questions.correctAnswer: Synchronous code blocks execution until complete, while asynchronous code allows other operations to run before completion

questions.question: What is the purpose of promises in JavaScript?
options: To replace all callbacks in JavaScript
To handle asynchronous operations and represent a future value or error
To make synchronous code run faster
To store data permanently in memory
questions.correctAnswer: To handle asynchronous operations and represent a future value or error

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of React hooks?
options: To replace Redux entirely
To allow state and lifecycle features in functional components
To improve CSS styling in React apps
To enforce class-based component usage
questions.correctAnswer: To allow state and lifecycle features in functional components

questions.question: What is the difference between useState and useReducer?
options: useState is for global state, useReducer is for local state
useReducer is better suited for complex state logic, while useState is simpler
useState is asynchronous while useReducer is synchronous
There is no difference between them
questions.correctAnswer: useReducer is better suited for complex state logic, while useState is simpler

questions.question: What does the dependency array in useEffect control?
options: Which components can access the effect
When the effect runs based on changes to specified values
How many times the component re-renders
Whether the component uses state or props
questions.correctAnswer: When the effect runs based on changes to specified values

questions.question: What is the virtual DOM in React?
options: A direct representation of the real DOM
A lightweight copy of the real DOM used to optimize updates
A browser API for DOM manipulation
A replacement for HTML
questions.correctAnswer: A lightweight copy of the real DOM used to optimize updates

questions.question: Why are keys important when rendering lists in React?
options: They improve CSS styling
They help React identify which elements have changed for efficient re-rendering
They allow components to share state
They are required for event handling
questions.correctAnswer: They help React identify which elements have changed for efficient re-rendering

questions.question: What is prop drilling in React?
options: Passing props deeply through multiple components unnecessarily
Using props to manage global state
A method of optimizing component rendering
A way to fetch data from APIs
questions.correctAnswer: Passing props deeply through multiple components unnecessarily

questions.question: What is the purpose of React.memo?
options: To memoize values inside a component
To prevent unnecessary re-renders of functional components based on props
To manage side effects
To handle asynchronous code
questions.correctAnswer: To prevent unnecessary re-renders of functional components based on props

questions.question: What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled components?
options: Controlled components manage state internally, uncontrolled rely on Redux
Controlled components use React state, uncontrolled components rely on the DOM
Uncontrolled components are deprecated
There is no difference
questions.correctAnswer: Controlled components use React state, uncontrolled components rely on the DOM

questions.question: What does useContext help avoid?
options: Using hooks in functional components
Prop drilling by providing a way to share values across components
Component re-renders entirely
Using external libraries
questions.correctAnswer: Prop drilling by providing a way to share values across components

questions.question: What is the main benefit of code splitting in React?
options: It reduces the number of components
It improves performance by loading code only when needed
It eliminates the need for state management
It simplifies JSX syntax
questions.correctAnswer: It improves performance by loading code only when needed

quizzes.title: Accessibility (a11y)
quizzes.description: Advanced accessibility questions covering inclusive design, ARIA usage, keyboard navigation, screen readers, and WCAG best practices.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of web accessibility?
options: To make websites visually appealing
To ensure websites are usable by people with disabilities
To improve website performance
To reduce development time
questions.correctAnswer: To ensure websites are usable by people with disabilities

questions.question: What does WCAG stand for?
options: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
Web Compliance and Accessibility Guide
Worldwide Content Accessibility Group
Web Coding Accessibility Guidelines
questions.correctAnswer: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines

questions.question: When should ARIA roles and attributes be used?
options: Always, instead of semantic HTML
Only when native HTML elements do not provide sufficient accessibility
Only for styling purposes
Only in JavaScript frameworks
questions.correctAnswer: Only when native HTML elements do not provide sufficient accessibility

questions.question: What is the purpose of the alt attribute on images?
options: To improve image loading speed
To provide alternative text for screen readers and when images fail to load
To style images with CSS
To define image dimensions
questions.correctAnswer: To provide alternative text for screen readers and when images fail to load

questions.question: Why is keyboard accessibility important?
options: It improves SEO rankings
Some users rely on keyboards or assistive technologies instead of a mouse
It makes animations smoother
It reduces page load time
questions.correctAnswer: Some users rely on keyboards or assistive technologies instead of a mouse

questions.question: What is a common issue with using only color to convey information?
options: It increases file size
It can make content inaccessible to users with color vision deficiencies
It slows down rendering
It breaks responsive layouts
questions.correctAnswer: It can make content inaccessible to users with color vision deficiencies

questions.question: What does 'focus' refer to in web accessibility?
options: The currently visible section of a page
The element currently selected for keyboard interaction
The main heading of the page
The element with the highest z-index
questions.correctAnswer: The element currently selected for keyboard interaction

questions.question: Which practice improves accessibility for form inputs?
options: Using placeholder text instead of labels
Associating labels explicitly with form controls
Removing all validation messages
Using only icons without text
questions.correctAnswer: Associating labels explicitly with form controls

questions.question: What is the purpose of skip navigation links?
options: To skip loading JavaScript files
To allow users to bypass repetitive navigation and jump to main content
To improve animation performance
To hide navigation menus
questions.correctAnswer: To allow users to bypass repetitive navigation and jump to main content

questions.question: What does a sufficient color contrast ratio ensure?
options: Faster rendering times
Better visibility and readability for users, including those with visual impairments
Improved JavaScript execution
Consistent layout spacing
questions.correctAnswer: Better visibility and readability for users, including those with visual impairments
